iterations.strategy: The hot time is dominated by the main scan because the current plan probes a full 8M-row `partsupp` hash whose 192 MB key/row arrays exceed the 88 MB LLC, and it also performs repeated random `orders` lookups even though `lineitem` is already ordered by `l_orderkey`. This revision uses strategy category A: restructure the plan so the `p_name LIKE '%green%'` filter is pushed into the `partsupp` build side, creating a green-only cache-resident join structure for about 384k rows, fuse supplier+nation into a tiny dense `suppkey -> nation_code` map, and exploit lineitem orderkey runs with contiguous thread partitions so order-year lookup becomes a seeded streaming/cached operation instead of a per-row random lookup.

The remaining gap comes from a storage-level mismatch: iteration 1 cut main-scan cost, but it still spends 14 ms in dim_filter and 24 ms rebuilding cache-friendly join state because the available persistent structures do not match Q9's direct-lookup access pattern. This revision uses strategy category E: persist three all-rows dense lookup arrays—a `suppkey -> nation_code` byte map, an `orderkey -> year_idx` byte map, and a `partkey -> 4 x (suppkey, supplycost_i32)` payload—so execution becomes a single sequential lineitem scan with only direct-array probes and per-thread array aggregation, eliminating the runtime partsupp repack and dimension build phases while keeping the hot working set near LLC size.
